description: Ground-truth expected verdicts and change kinds for all abicheck example cases. Single source of truth — kept in sync with tests/test_example_autodiscovery.py::EXPECTED. expected_kinds lists the ChangeKind values that MUST appear in the diff result (subset check). expected_absent_kinds lists kinds that MUST NOT appear (false positive guard). min_evidence is the minimum evidence layer (L0 binary / L1 +debug / L2 +headers / L3 +build / L4 +source) at which abicheck reaches the correct verdict — derived by scripts/evidence_tiers.py and validated by tests/test_evidence_tiers.py.

cross_references.verdict_classification: abicheck/checker_policy.py — BREAKING_KINDS, API_BREAK_KINDS, RISK_KINDS, COMPATIBLE_KINDS (= ADDITION_KINDS ∪ QUALITY_KINDS)
cross_references.category_mapping: breaking → BREAKING_KINDS | api_break → API_BREAK_KINDS | risk → RISK_KINDS | addition → ADDITION_KINDS | quality → QUALITY_KINDS | no_change → (empty)
cross_references.evidence_tiers: scripts/evidence_tiers.py — EVIDENCE_TIER_BY_KIND + KINDLESS_CASE_TIER compute min_evidence; benchmark_comparison.py --evidence-tiers runs the catalog per tier
cross_references.test_autodiscovery: tests/test_example_autodiscovery.py — reads EXPECTED from this file
cross_references.test_evidence_tiers: tests/test_evidence_tiers.py — keeps min_evidence in sync with scripts/evidence_tiers.py
cross_references.test_manual: tests/test_abi_examples.py — hardcoded expectations for cases 01-18 (must stay in sync)
cross_references.examples_readme: examples/README.md — case index table (must stay in sync)

case15_noexcept_change.note: noexcept change itself is COMPATIBLE; GLIBCXX version requirement is deployment risk

case20_enum_member_value_changed.reason: regular enum member value changes remain breaking; only named sentinel patterns are downgraded

case32_param_defaults.description: Parameter default-value changes: connect() default 30->60 (changed), configure()'s verbose loses its default (removed -> source break), disconnect() gains a default (compatible). The mangled symbols are unchanged so binaries keep running; the break is source-only and detectable only in header mode (castxml Argument `default` attribute). Previously a documented gap (NO_CHANGE) until default-arg extraction was wired up.
case32_param_defaults.note: castxml on macOS/Homebrew does not emit the `default=` attribute on <Argument> elements, so the param_default_value_* detection only fires with the Linux castxml build. Scoped to linux to avoid a platform-dependent castxml gap (constant/finality extraction is unaffected and stays cross-platform).

case42_type_alignment_changed.description: Struct alignment changed via __attribute__((aligned(8))) to aligned(64) — stack allocation and array stride break (TYPE_ALIGNMENT_CHANGED)
case42_type_alignment_changed.known_gap: macOS: Tool returns NO_CHANGE — without castxml, Mach-O mode captures only exported symbols; type-level alignment changes are invisible

case43_base_class_member_added.description: Data member added to base class shifts derived class layout (libabigail: Added_Base_Class_Data_Member)

case44_cyclic_type_member_added.description: Member added to a self-referential (cyclic) struct — sizeof changes (libabigail: Added_Non_Virtual_Member_Variable in cyclic type)

case45_multi_dim_array_change.description: Multi-dimensional array element type changed float->double — size doubles (libabigail: Subrange_Change)

case46_pointer_chain_type_change.description: Pointer chain ultimate type changes int->long through two indirection levels (libabigail: Function_Return_Type_Change indirect)

case47_inline_to_outlined.description: Inline method moved out-of-line: new exported symbol appears (FUNC_ADDED, libabigail: inline->outlined)

case48_leaf_struct_through_pointer.description: Leaf struct gains field; propagates through embedding in Container — layout breaks (libabigail: Leaf_Type_Change)

case49_executable_stack.description: Library linked with -Wl,-z,execstack has GNU_STACK RWE — disables NX protection (bad practice — security)

case50_soname_inconsistent.description: SONAME present but set to wrong major version — bakes wrong DT_NEEDED into consumers (bad practice — packaging/upgrade hazard)

case51_protected_visibility.description: Symbol visibility changed DEFAULT→PROTECTED — exported but no longer interposable (compatible, interposition policy concern)

case52_rpath_leak.description: Library has hardcoded build-directory path in DT_RUNPATH — non-portable deployment (bad practice — packaging)

case53_namespace_pollution.description: Library exports generic symbol names (init, process, cleanup) without prefix — collision risk (bad practice — API design)

case54_used_reserved_field.description: Reserved placeholder fields (__reserved1, __reserved2) renamed to meaningful names at same offsets — layout unchanged (USED_RESERVED_FIELD)
case54_used_reserved_field.known_gap: macOS: Tool returns NO_CHANGE — without castxml, Mach-O mode captures only exported symbols; struct field renames are invisible

case55_type_kind_changed.description: Type changed from struct to union — fields overlap instead of sequential layout (TYPE_KIND_CHANGED)
case55_type_kind_changed.known_gap: macOS: Tool returns NO_CHANGE — without castxml, Mach-O mode captures only exported symbols; struct-to-union type kind change is invisible

case56_struct_packing_changed.description: Struct packing changed via pragma pack(1) — field offsets and sizeof change (STRUCT_PACKING_CHANGED)
case56_struct_packing_changed.known_gap: macOS: Tool returns NO_CHANGE — without castxml, Mach-O mode captures only exported symbols; packing/layout changes are invisible

case57_enum_underlying_size_changed.description: Enum underlying type grows from int to long due to large sentinel value — sizeof changes (ENUM_UNDERLYING_SIZE_CHANGED)
case57_enum_underlying_size_changed.known_gap: macOS: Tool returns NO_CHANGE — without castxml, Mach-O mode captures only exported symbols; enum underlying size changes are invisible

case58_var_removed.description: Global variable lib_debug_level removed from export table (VAR_REMOVED)
case58_var_removed.known_gap: macOS: Mach-O export table does not distinguish functions from variables, so lib_debug_level can be detected as func_removed_elf_only instead of var_removed; both are breaking under strict binary policy.

case59_func_became_inline.description: Functions moved from outlined to static inline in header — symbols disappear from .dynsym (FUNC_REMOVED, inverse of case47)

case60_base_class_position_changed.description: Multiple inheritance base class order swapped — subobject offsets and vtable pointers change (BASE_CLASS_POSITION_CHANGED)
case60_base_class_position_changed.known_gap: macOS: Tool returns NO_CHANGE — no headers provided; Mach-O mode captures only exported symbols; base class layout changes are invisible

case61_var_added.description: New global variable lib_build_number added — purely additive (VAR_ADDED)

case62_type_field_added_compatible.description: Field added to opaque struct (callers use pointer only) — layout change invisible to consumers (TYPE_FIELD_ADDED compatible)

case63_bitfield_changed.description: Bitfield width changed in struct — all subsequent bitfields shift to different bit positions (FIELD_BITFIELD_CHANGED)

case64_calling_convention_changed.description: Function calling convention changed from default System V to ms_abi — parameters passed in wrong registers (CALLING_CONVENTION_CHANGED)
case64_calling_convention_changed.known_gap: GCC does not emit DW_AT_calling_convention for __attribute__((ms_abi)); detection requires Clang

case65_symbol_version_removed.description: ELF symbol version node CRYPTO_1.0 removed — old binaries linked against that version fail at load time (SYMBOL_VERSION_DEFINED_REMOVED)

case66_language_linkage_changed.description: extern "C" removed from C++ functions — symbol names change from unmangled to C++ mangled (FUNC_LANGUAGE_LINKAGE_CHANGED)

case67_tls_var_size_changed.description: Thread-local variable ErrorCtx grows from 68 to 72 bytes due to inserted field — struct field offsets shift, causing consumers to read wrong data (TLS_VAR_SIZE_CHANGED)

case68_virtual_method_added.description: Virtual method added to previously non-virtual class — vtable pointer inserted, sizeof grows, all field offsets shift (FUNC_VIRTUAL_ADDED)

case69_trivial_to_nontrivial.known_gap: Windows MSVC x64: structs >8 bytes are always passed by pointer regardless of triviality, so the calling convention does not change
case69_trivial_to_nontrivial.description: Trivially-copyable struct gains user-defined destructor — calling convention changes from register passing to hidden pointer (VALUE_ABI_TRAIT_CHANGED)

case70_flexible_array_member_changed.description: Flexible array member element type changed float → double — dynamic allocation size and element access offsets break while sizeof(Packet) stays the same (FLEXIBLE_ARRAY_MEMBER_CHANGED)

case71_inline_namespace_moved.description: Symbols moved from inline namespace v1 to v2 — mangled names change so old binaries fail to resolve symbols (INLINE_NAMESPACE_MOVED)

case72_covariant_return_changed.description: Intermediate class inserted into hierarchy — vtable layout shifts, object size changes, covariant return type changes from Circle* to Drawable* (TYPE_BASE_CHANGED)

case73_typedef_underlying_changed.description: Typedef handle_t underlying type changed from int to void* — sizeof changes from 4 to 8 bytes, all functions using the typedef inherit the break (TYPEDEF_BASE_CHANGED)

case74_detail_base_class_changed.description: Internal-namespace leak: mylib::detail::descriptor_base gains a field; public mylib::knn_descriptor inherits from it, so sizeof/layout of the public class changes. Detected by existing type_size_changed/struct_field_added, escalated by internal_type_leaks_via_public_api.

case75_detail_embedded_by_value.description: Internal-namespace leak: mylib::detail::table_impl gains a field; public mylib::table embeds it by value, so sizeof(table) grows. Detected by existing struct_field_added on detail::table_impl, escalated by internal_type_leaks_via_public_api.

case76_detail_pimpl_vtable_changed.known_gap: macOS / Windows: vtable break is currently only detectable via symbol_size_changed on the ELF vtable symbol; Mach-O / PE export tables carry no symbol size, and castxml emits vtable_index=None for all virtuals so the structural diff collapses to a single slot — neither signal fires on these platforms
case76_detail_pimpl_vtable_changed.description: Internal-namespace leak: mylib::detail::algorithm_iface gains a virtual method inserted mid-vtable; public mylib::svm_algorithm inherits from it, so vtable slot indices shift for all consumers. Detected by existing type_vtable_changed / func_virtual_added, escalated by internal_type_leaks_via_public_api.

case77_detail_templated_base_changed.description: Internal-namespace leak through a templated detail:: base: mylib::detail::descriptor_base<Task> gains a field, growing every knn_descriptor<Task> instantiation. Verifies the leak reachability walker follows template-instantiation edges into detail::.

case79_missing_template_instantiation.description: Header advertises descriptor<float> and descriptor<double>; v2 library ships only the float instantiation. Header is unchanged so source compiles, link/load fails with undefined symbol. Emits INSTANTIATION_MISSING_FROM_BINARY.

case80_pimpl_shared_to_unique.known_gap: Windows: std::shared_ptr / std::unique_ptr layout names differ between MSVC STL and libstdc++/libc++, so the type-name-based detection signal is not portable as-shipped. Tracked for a follow-up that normalises smart-pointer template-arg names across stdlibs.
case80_pimpl_shared_to_unique.description: Pimpl alias switched from shared_ptr<T> to unique_ptr<T>. The type of descriptor::impl_ changed; inline accessors re-mangle, destruction model changes (no atomic refcount), copy semantics flip from refcounted-share to move-only. Detected by existing type_field_type_changed escalated via internal_type_leaks_via_public_api.

case81_serialization_tag_reassigned.known_gap: Windows: the v1.h fixture includes ``<cstdint>`` for ``std::uint64_t``. On the GitHub Actions Windows runner, castxml's bundled Clang frontend can't parse mingw64 GCC 15.2.0's ``bits/c++config.h`` (it uses the C++23 ``bf16`` floating-point literal suffix: ``typedef __decltype(0.0bf16) __bfloat16_t;``). This is a toolchain incompatibility, not an issue with the detector or the example — castxml dump fails before the detector runs. Same root cause as case80 / case89.
case81_serialization_tag_reassigned.description: DAAL-style serialization tag IDs swapped values between knn_model and linear_regression. Symbols, types, layout all unchanged — every conventional ABI check passes — but saved models silently deserialize as the wrong class. Emits SERIALIZATION_TAG_CHANGED.

case82_sycl_overload_set_removed.description: DPC++ build withdrawn between releases — every sycl::queue&-taking overload disappears across multiple algorithms. Reported as one grouped SYCL_OVERLOAD_SET_REMOVED finding instead of N independent func_removed.

case83_cpu_dispatch_isa_dropped.description: AVX-512 ISA tier dropped: every *_avx512_* symbol removed across all algorithms. Dispatcher-using callers unaffected; ISA-pinned callers break. Reported as one grouped CPU_DISPATCH_ISA_DROPPED finding (RISK_KINDS).

case84_bundle_soname_skew.reason: Bundle case — no single-pair v1.cpp/v2.cpp layout; uses gen_bundle.sh to produce v1/ and v2/ directories. Validated end-to-end through `compare-release` in tests/test_bundle.py::TestCompareReleaseBundleE2E::test_compare_release_emits_soname_skew_for_case84; detect_bundle_soname_skew() also unit-tested in tests/test_cpp_pattern_detectors.py.
case84_bundle_soname_skew.description: Multi-library bundle SONAME skew: libonedal_core and libonedal_dpc bump SONAME from .so.1 to .so.2 but libonedal_thread keeps .so.1 by accident. Per-library checks pass; cohort-level invariant fails. Emits BUNDLE_SONAME_SKEW.

case86_tag_struct_renamed.description: Empty tag struct method::brute_force renamed to method::search_brute. No layout signal (empty type), but every explicit descriptor<method::brute_force, ...> instantiation re-mangles. Emits TAG_TYPE_RENAMED correlating the empty type rename with the symbol-name shift.

case87_default_template_arg_changed.description: Default template argument changed (Distance = minkowski_distance → euclidean_distance). Consumer source compiles unchanged but substituted instantiation type differs, producing a different mangled symbol. Emits DEFAULT_TEMPLATE_ARG_CHANGED. Unlike case32 (function default param, NO_CHANGE), template defaults are part of the substituted type.

case89_inline_accessor_renamed_pimpl_mem.known_gap: Windows: the public class holds std::shared_ptr<detail::descriptor_impl> which renders differently in MSVC PDB/STL field-type strings, so the internal-leak reachability heuristic that ties the inline accessor to the renamed detail:: member does not fire as-shipped.
case89_inline_accessor_renamed_pimpl_mem.description: Public inline accessor reaches into detail::descriptor_impl::class_count_ by name. v2 renames the field to n_classes_ and updates the inline body in lockstep, but consumers compiled against v1.h have the OLD field name baked into their inline accessor bodies. Emits INLINE_BODY_REFERENCES_RENAMED_MEMBER.

case90_bundle_intra_dep_removed.description: Bundle (ADR-023): libcore removes core_mul; sibling libalgo still imports it (DT_NEEDED libcore.so.1 + U core_mul). Per-library compare reports libalgo NO_CHANGE; the bundle layer catches the dangling intra-bundle import.

case91_bundle_intra_signature_drift.description: Bundle (ADR-023): libcore changes core_add(int,int) -> core_add(long,long). extern C, mangled name unchanged. libalgo binary is byte-identical between versions but its call sites still use the int calling convention. Bundle layer flags libalgo as bundle_intra_dep_signature_changed.

case92_bundle_provider_changed.description: Bundle (ADR-023): shared_util moves from libcore to libutil between releases. The bundle's union of exported symbols is unchanged but DT_NEEDED contracts shift; downstream binaries that only linked -lcore now miss the symbol. Per-library worst-of reports BREAKING; the bundle layer adds COMPATIBLE_WITH_RISK provider-change finding.

case93_bundle_manifest_drift.description: Bundle (ADR-023): manifest.yaml promises four train_* instantiations; the new bundle silently drops train_double_sparse. With --manifest, the bundle layer emits bundle_manifest_instantiation_removed. Without --manifest, the per-library func_removed already covers it but cannot distinguish 'promised' from 'incidental'.

case94_empty_tag_gained_state.description: An empty tag struct (mirrors tbb::auto_partitioner) gains a member; sizeof grows from 1 to 8. Any consumer that passed the tag by value into a header-inline algorithm interface now writes an undersized argument into a wider slot. Detected by the existing type_size_changed / type_field_added pair.

case95_allocator_nested_typedef_removed.description: An allocator-style class drops the historical STL nested-typedef set (value_type, pointer, reference, size_type, difference_type). Exported member symbols are unchanged so binaries still link, but consumer source using `typename Alloc::value_type` etc. fails to compile. Detected via `typedef_removed` (BREAKING per change_registry). Demonstrates the new `member_name` suppression selector — combined with `change_kind: typedef_removed`, it scopes the inevitable noise from removing a uniform set of nested aliases.

case96_hidden_friend_removed.description: A non-member operator (`operator==`) declared as an in-class `friend` with an inline body (a 'hidden friend') is removed in v2. Consumers that wrote `a == b` resolved the call via ADL to the friend; with the declaration gone, ADL finds nothing and the source no longer compiles. The .so symbol set is unchanged because inline hidden friends typically have no external symbol. Detected via the new HIDDEN_FRIEND_REMOVED ChangeKind, derived from castxml's `befriending` attribute on the Class/Struct element.

case105_concept_tightening.known_gap: castxml emits C++20 `concept` declarations as <Unimplemented kind="Concept"/> with no name and no body, and drops the templates that use them. There is no way to detect concept tightening from the castxml dump path; the case is preserved as a regression fixture until the header-AST capture path on the roadmap lands (a libclang-based extractor sitting alongside castxml that captures each concept's constrained parameters and `requires`-expression body). abicheck currently returns NO_CHANGE on this case.
case105_concept_tightening.description: A C++20 `concept` (`Addable`) gains an additional requirement (default-constructibility). The already-shipped `sum<int>` instantiation keeps the same mangled symbol so previously-compiled binaries keep linking, but consumers instantiating `sum<T>` for types that fail the tightened constraint no longer compile. Concept tightening is detection-blocked by castxml at present; see `known_gap`.

case106_ctor_became_explicit.known_gap: castxml does not emit the `explicit` attribute on <Converter> elements (user-defined conversion operators), and does not emit a mangled name for user-declared constructors. The CTOR_EXPLICIT_ADDED detector is therefore not exercised end-to-end by the default castxml-based dump path on Linux/macOS — only via DWARF-only mode. The detector itself is validated by tests/test_explicit_ctor.py with synthetic snapshots.
case106_ctor_became_explicit.description: A converting conversion operator on a public handle type (mirrors `operator int()` on a oneTBB handle) gains the `explicit` specifier. The mangled name is unchanged so previously-compiled binaries still link, but any source code that wrote `int n = ta;` or relied on implicit conversion at a call site fails to compile against v2. Detected by the new CTOR_EXPLICIT_ADDED ChangeKind, derived from DW_AT_explicit on DW_TAG_subprogram (and `explicit="1"` on castxml Method/Constructor — but not Converter, see known_gap).

case107_task_scheduler_init_removed.description: Named regression fixture for oneTBB's largest historical ABI break: tbb::task_scheduler_init was removed in oneTBB 2021.1. The entire publicly-exported class and all of its members disappear. Detected by the existing FUNC_REMOVED / TYPE_REMOVED detectors.

case108_task_class_removed.description: Named regression fixture for the oneTBB 2021.1 removal of the polymorphic `tbb::task` base class. Removing a publicly-derivable polymorphic base destroys every user subclass's vtable, breaks RTTI across DSOs, and undefines the typeinfo symbol. Detected by existing FUNC_REMOVED / TYPE_REMOVED detectors.

case109_flow_graph_policy_renames.description: Header-only policy-tag types used as template parameters for `function_node` are renamed wholesale (queueing/rejecting → buffering_policy/backpressure_policy) and the instantiation-anchor typedef is dropped. The example deliberately keeps v1.cpp / v2.cpp byte-identical (no template instantiations) so the .so symbol table is unchanged across the version pair; any rebuild against v2 headers still fails. Detected via `typedef_removed` + `type_removed` (both BREAKING per change_registry). Member of the oneTBB regression suite; mirrors the 2021 flow::graph policy reshuffle.

case110_concurrent_unordered_map_api_dri.description: Public member function `insert` loses one of its parameters between versions. The old mangled symbol disappears, the new overload has a different mangled name — both source and link break. Member of the oneTBB regression suite; mirrors historical signature tightenings on `tbb::concurrent_unordered_map`.

case111_enumerable_thread_specific_lambd.known_gap: Two gaps stack here. (1) castxml does not emit mangled names for user-declared constructors (same gap as case106 / case78), so the added second ctor cannot be observed as FUNC_ADDED end-to-end — abicheck currently returns NO_CHANGE on this case. (2) Even with the FUNC_ADDED in place, constructor overload ambiguity at consumer call sites cannot be detected from library snapshots alone; that needs the castxml header-AST capture path on the roadmap (concept tightening / hidden friends). The case is preserved as a regression fixture for whenever either gap is closed.
case111_enumerable_thread_specific_lambd.description: A second constructor overload accepting `std::function<int()>` is added next to the existing `int`-form constructor. By itself this is a pure addition — existing call sites that pass an int still resolve to the original constructor. The risk: brace-initialization and generic callable arguments can become ambiguous at downstream call sites. Member of the oneTBB regression suite.

case78_task_arena_attach_tag.known_gap: Two detector gaps stack on this case in the castxml-driven pipeline: (1) castxml does not emit mangled names for user-declared constructors (same gap as case106), so the v1 ctor `task_arena(attach_mode_t)` collapses with all other ctors under mangled='task_arena' and FUNC_REMOVED never fires; (2) abicheck's enum diff defers wholly-removed enums to TYPE_REMOVED, but TYPE_REMOVED only iterates `snap.types`, never `snap.enums`, so `attach_mode_t` removal goes silently undetected. End-to-end the verdict comes out COMPATIBLE (only the new nested `attach` struct is observed as TYPE_ADDED). The case is preserved as a regression fixture for whenever either gap is closed.
case78_task_arena_attach_tag.description: An `attach_mode_t` enum + matching constructor are replaced by an empty tag struct `task_arena::attach` + tag-dispatched constructor. The old enum, its members, and the old constructor's mangled symbol are all removed. Member of the oneTBB regression suite; mirrors the 2021 `task_arena::attach` tag introduction.

case85_internal_template_signature_chang.description: Internal helper template `lib::__detail::walk<T>` is dispatched through by a public inline algorithm `sum_range`. Between v1 and v2 the explicit instantiation set shifts (`walk<float>` removed, `walk<double>` added). Consumers compiled against v1 baked `walk<float>` into their symbol table; against v2 they fail to link. Function-template analogue of internal_type_leaks_via_public_api from PR #238.

case88_cpo_kind_changed.description: Public name `lib::sort` is published in v1 as a free function and in v2 as a customisation point object (constexpr variable of an unspecified function-object class type). Call syntax keeps working but decltype, extern templates, and trait specialisations break.

case97_api_depends_on_consumer_env.known_gap: The single-config build harness does not exercise the matrix-aware API_DEPENDS_ON_CONSUMER_ENV detector — that detector consumes a MatrixSnapshot (multiple per-configuration snapshots) which is only produced by the `abicheck probe` subcommand (not wired into the default `compare` CLI yet). In the current single-config build, castxml dumps v1.h and v2.h WITHOUT USE_FEATURE defined, so the declared-function set is identical between v1 and v2; the underlying lib v1 still exports `extended` because v1.cpp `#define USE_FEATURE 1` before include — that surfaces as FUNC_REMOVED_ELF_ONLY in per-binary comparison. The case is preserved as documentation of the macro-conditioned shape; the matrix detector remains the richer explanation channel.
case97_api_depends_on_consumer_env.description: Header declares `lib::extended()` only when the consumer defines `USE_FEATURE`. The v1 build defines the macro and exports extended(); v2 builds without it. The matrix-aware API_DEPENDS_ON_CONSUMER_ENV finding is the channel that exposes this when both macro states are probed.

case98_cxx_standard_floor_raised.known_gap: The C++ standard floor signal is invisible to a per-binary diff (symbol/type set is unchanged between v1 and v2). The matrix-aware CXX_STANDARD_FLOOR_RAISED detector (emitted by the probe harness when fed per-configuration cxx_std metadata) is the only correct channel for this signal. The CMakeLists.txt builds v2 with -std=c++20 to embed a post-C++17 contract via build configuration, but the public declaration set is intentionally identical to v1's so the case still produces NO_CHANGE under the current single-config pipeline.
case98_cxx_standard_floor_raised.description: v1 header works under C++17; v2 is built with -std=c++20 but declares the same public surface as v1. Per-binary diff is NO_CHANGE; the source-level break for C++17 consumers only surfaces via the matrix-aware CXX_STANDARD_FLOOR_RAISED detector.

case99_experimental_graduated.description: A declaration lived only under lib::experimental:: in v1 and is published at the stable lib:: name in v2 while the experimental alias is retained. The dedicated EXPERIMENTAL_GRADUATED finding makes the migration event visible; the underlying signal is also a plain func_added for the stable name.

case100_experimental_removed_without_rep.description: A declaration only ever lived under lib::experimental:: in v1 and is removed from v2 with no stable-namespace replacement. The dedicated EXPERIMENTAL_REMOVED_WITHOUT_REPLACEMENT finding tells reviewers there is no migration target; the underlying mangled symbol disappearance is also reported as a plain func_removed.

case101_inline_namespace_version_bumped.description: Library uses a versioned inline namespace `_V1` in v1 and bumps to `_V2` in v2. Declarations look identical to consumers so source keeps compiling, but every freshly built TU emits mangled symbols carrying the new version segment — old and new TUs of the same program ODR-violate. Specialisation of the existing inline_namespace_moved that fires from declared-name evidence so it works on header-only inputs and single-symbol bumps.

case102_frozen_runtime_signature_changed.description: An extern "C" runtime entry inside a contractually-frozen namespace (mirrors oneTBB's `detail::r1`) has its parameter and return types widened in place. The exported symbol name is unchanged but callers built against v1 pass int into what is now a long slot. Detected by the existing FUNC_PARAMS_CHANGED / FUNC_RETURN_CHANGED detectors; the new `frozen_namespaces:` policy field additionally tags the finding via Change.frozen_namespace_violation and refuses any `overrides:` entry that would downgrade it. See policy.yaml in the case directory.

case103_toolchain_flag_drift.description: Public surface and every exported symbol are identical between v1 and v2; only the ABI-affecting compiler flag set differs (v2 adds -fshort-enums). The toolchain-flag drift detector parses DW_AT_producer and reports the mismatch as a compatible, informational quality signal — a leading indicator that the two builds were made under different ABI assumptions, before that drift turns into a real layout break on a library that exposes enums/packed structs/a different data model.

case104_glibcxx_dual_abi_flip.description: Identical C++ source built once with _GLIBCXX_USE_CXX11_ABI=0 (v1) and once with =1 (v2). GNU libstdc++ ships two incompatible std::string/std::list ABIs selected by this macro, so every std::string-bearing exported symbol gets a different mangled name (std::__cxx11::basic_string vs the legacy std::basic_string). abicheck recognises the mass-churn signature and collapses it into a single glibcxx_dual_abi_flip_detected diagnostic alongside the underlying func_removed/func_added evidence; verdict is BREAKING. GNU libstdc++ only.

case112_lp64_ilp64.description: A BLAS/LAPACK-style integer interface (as shipped by e.g. oneMKL) switched LP64 (INT=int, 32-bit) to ILP64 (INT=long, 64-bit). A large fraction of public integer parameters/returns flip width together and the integer typedef resizes, the signature of an LP64/ILP64 model switch.

case113_abi_tag_changed.description: A single C++ symbol loses its Itanium [abi:cxx11] ABI tag ([[gnu::abi_tag]]). The demangled name is unchanged but the mangled symbol (B5cxx11 component) differs, so consumers linked against the tagged symbol fail to resolve it.

case114_char8t_migration.description: A public UTF-8 API migrated from char-family spellings to C++20 char8_t on parameters and a struct field. char8_t is a distinct type (mangling Du), so the mangled symbols change and old binaries fail to resolve them.

case115_bit_int_width_changed.description: A public C23 _BitInt(64) accumulator widened to _BitInt(128) across a struct field, a by-value parameter, and a return type. The bit width is part of the type, so storage size and calling-convention treatment change. (128 is clang's maximum _BitInt width, keeping the case portable.)

case116_atomic_qualifier_changed.description: A public struct field and a return type gained the C11 _Atomic qualifier. Per WG14 the size/alignment of an _Atomic-qualified type may differ from the unqualified type and varies across compilers, so layout and the return ABI diverge.

case117_no_unique_address.description: An empty member gained [[no_unique_address]] in C++20, letting the compiler overlay it with the next member. No dedicated ChangeKind is needed: the overlay shows up as the existing type_size_changed / type_field_offset_changed layout kinds.

case121_kernel_btf_struct_field_added.description: Linux kernel BTF: a vmlinux struct (task_state) grows from 2 to 3 fields between kernel releases, changing sizeof 8→12. An out-of-tree module / eBPF CO-RE program built against the old layout accesses wrong offsets. Committed BTF blobs (v1.btf/v2.btf) are parsed by parse_btf_from_bytes and diffed through the same layout detectors as DWARF.
case121_kernel_btf_struct_field_added.reason: committed BTF blobs (not compilable C/C++ source); validated end-to-end via tests/test_workflow_kernel_accel.py::test_committed_btf_example_matches_ground_truth and the gcc -gbtf integration fixture

case122_template_signature_uninstantiate.known_gap: The library ships the function template header-only and instantiates nothing, so no symbol is emitted and the v1/v2 binaries are byte-identical. castxml also does not emit uninstantiated template declarations into its AST output, so the parameter-type change (int -> long) is invisible in BOTH object/DWARF mode and header/castxml mode. This is the fundamental boundary of comparing built artifacts: code that never becomes a symbol leaves no trace a binary or castxml comparison can observe. A pure source-AST (header-diff) tool can see it; the approaches are complementary.
case122_template_signature_uninstantiate.description: An uninstantiated function template's parameter types change (int -> long). A real source/API break for consumers of the template, but undetectable by abicheck in any mode — documented limitation demonstrating that some C++-only changes leave nothing in the objects to compare.

case123_default_argument_removed.description: A function's default argument is removed in v2. The mangled symbol is identical (defaults don't affect mangling), so binaries keep running, but callers relying on the default no longer compile. Detected only in header mode via the param_default_value_removed ChangeKind (castxml exposes the Argument `default` attribute); invisible in object/DWARF mode.
case123_default_argument_removed.note: castxml on macOS/Homebrew does not emit the `default=` attribute on <Argument> elements, so the param_default_value_* detection only fires with the Linux castxml build. Scoped to linux to avoid a platform-dependent castxml gap (constant/finality extraction is unaffected and stays cross-platform).

case124_header_constant_value_changed.description: A public constexpr constant changes value (8 -> 16). The value is baked into consumers at compile time, so mixing header versions disagrees on sizes; yet the constant has internal linkage (no symbol), so the binaries are identical and object/DWARF comparison sees nothing. Detected only in header mode via constant_changed: the dumper extracts const/constexpr initializers declared in the provided public headers (castxml `init` attribute). Plain #define macros remain invisible (castxml emits no macros).

case125_class_became_final.description: A public, extensible base class gains the `final` specifier in v2. Layout, vtable and mangled names are unchanged, so already-compiled binaries keep running, but any consumer that derives from it no longer compiles. Detected via the new TYPE_BECAME_FINAL ChangeKind, which reads the `final` class-key from castxml header analysis. Invisible in object/DWARF mode (the binaries are ABI-identical), demonstrating why source/header analysis is required for the full API picture.

case126_sycl_device_impl_ptr.description: Distilled from intel/llvm PR #20821 (the `abi-break`-labelled change that swapped `std::shared_ptr<device_impl>` for a raw `device_impl*` inside `sycl::device`, surfacing as issue #20915 / fix #21028). The shared_ptr member is two pointers (16 bytes) on LP64; the raw pointer is one (8 bytes), so sizeof(sycl::device) shrinks 16->8 — an ABI break for every consumer that holds a device by value or embeds it. The method mangled names are unchanged, so a pure exported-symbol dump (sycl_symbols_*.dump) does not see the layout change directly; abicheck's type/layout diff reports the root cause as TYPE_SIZE_CHANGED. Built with -fvisibility-inlines-hidden so the diff shows the public ABI break rather than libstdc++ template noise, mirroring how libsycl is built.

case127_data_object_size_changed.description: An exported global data object (config_table) grows from int[16] (64 bytes) to int[32] (128 bytes), changing its ELF symbol st_size. Consumers that reference the object get a copy relocation sized for the v1 definition, so after the upgrade library code that indexes the new slots writes past the consumer's fixed-size copy — silent out-of-bounds corruption. This is the static-variable-size regression the glibc testsuite guards and that Apple's dynamic-library guidelines classify as a major change; detected at L0 from the symbol table alone.

case128_symbol_binding_strengthened.description: The exported function helper changes from weak (STB_WEAK) to strong/global (STB_GLOBAL) binding. This is backward compatible: every binary that resolved to the weak definition still resolves to the same address, and the only difference is that the symbol can no longer be silently overridden by another strong definition. It is the benign counterpart to case27 (binding weakened, which is risky). Detected at L0 from the symbol table.
